Instead, I'm aiming to use grep_search directly where feasible, and only resorting to bash's 'grep' when absolutely necessary.\n\n\n"}]}}],"usageMetadata": {"promptTokenCount": 63212,"totalTokenCount": 63212},"modelVersion": "gemini-pro-default","responseId": "o7MLavbTHJjM4-EPzuvwmAo"},"traceId": "aec5d1056c3376b4","metadata": {}}

data: {"response": {"candidates": [{"content": {"role": "model","parts": [{"thought": true,"text": "**Clarifying Scope Boundaries**\n\nI'm now clarifying scope boundaries within the `flushPending` function to correctly identify its closure. My investigation has led me to scrutinize where the function is supposed to close. I'm focusing on line 107 in `view_file` to understand how the nested structures interact. Specifically, I need to understand whether the closing brace at line 107 properly closes the `flushPending` function defined on line 33, or if an earlier closure is intended.\n\n\n"}]}}],"usageMetadata": {"promptTokenCount": 63212,"totalTokenCount": 63212},"modelVersion": "gemini-pro-default","responseId": "o7MLavbTHJjM4-EPzuvwmAo"},"traceId": "aec5d1056c3376b4","metadata": {}}

data: {"response": {"candidates": [{"content": {"role": "model","parts": [{"thoughtSignature": "","functionCall": {"name": "write_to_file","args": {"TargetFile": "f:\\AUDIRA-ROUTET\\check_braces.js","IsArtifact": false,"Description": "Trace braces","CodeContent": "const fs = require('fs');\nconst content = fs.readFileSync('f:/AUDIRA-ROUTET/open-sse/translator/request/openai-to-kiro.js', 'utf8');\nconst stack = [];\nconst lines = content.split('\\n');\nfor (let i = 0; i \u003c lines.length; i++) {\n  let line = lines[i];\n  \n  line = line.replace(/\"[^\"]*\"/g, '\"\"');\n  line = line.replace(/'[^']*'/g, \"''\");\n  line = line.replace(/`[^`]*`/g, \"``\");\n  line = line.split('//')[0];\n\n  for (let j = 0; j \u003c line.length; j++) {\n    const char = line[j];\n    if (char === '{') {\n      stack.push(i + 1);\n    }\n    if (char === '}') {\n      const closed = stack.pop();\n      if (closed \u003e= 33 && i + 1 \u003c= 107) {\n        console.log(`Line ${i + 1} '}' closes brace from line ${closed}`);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n","Overwrite": true},"id": "fd52to2p"}}]}}],"usageMetadata": {"promptTokenCount": 63212,"candidatesTokenCount": 335,"totalTokenCount": 64013,"thoughtsTokenCount": 466},"modelVersion": "gemini-pro-default","responseId": "o7MLavbTHJjM4-EPzuvwmAo"},"traceId": "aec5d1056c3376b4","metadata": {}}
